mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand strength, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during economic expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative 5-day forward returns during expansion phases, or the signal loses predictive power when GDPC1 growth exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained federal funds rates above 4.5% signal restrictive monetary policy that compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20d returns in > 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds the 4.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in retail sales reflect immediate consumer spending strength, which directly supports corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS increases fail to predict positive 5-day forward returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month declines in industrial production signal manufacturing contraction that reduces corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines produce positive 20d returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.
